title: Prionitis timbalii
taxonomy: Prionitis timbalii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. This plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Prionitis timbalii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Gentianales. It belongs to the family Rubiaceae due to its characteristic woody stems and clusters of small, white flowers. The classification of Prionitis timbalii is an ongoing area of research and may be subject to revision as new information becomes available.
